prompt.name: pattern-copy

content.thinking: Let me start by understanding the project structure and the real app's runtime environment before writing any Storybook configuration or stories.

input.prompt:
Very thoroughly explore this codebase to understand the full runtime environment. I need to know:

1. The entry point (main.tsx/index.tsx) and what it imports (CSS, providers, etc.)
2. App.tsx - the full provider tree and routing setup
3. All providers/contexts used
4. All CSS files imported at the root level
5. All hooks that fetch data (what URLs, what APIs)
6. Any browser state reads (localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies)
7. Any portal usage (createPortal, modal roots, etc.)
8. All pages/routes and their components
9. The project structure - what components exist at various levels
10. Package.json dependencies
11. Any existing Storybook configuration in .storybook/
12. The tsconfig and vite config
13. Any existing tests that show usage patterns

Start from the entry point and follow imports deeply. Read the actual file contents, don't just list files. I need comprehensive understanding of every provider, every fetch call, every piece of browser state, and every portal root.

1→import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
     2→import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
     3→import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
     4→import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'
     5→
     6→import { store } from './app-state'
     7→import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
     8→import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
     9→import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'
    10→
    11→export default function App() {
    12→  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
    13→  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme
    14→
    15→  return (
    16→    <Router>
    17→      <StoreProvider store={store}>
    18→        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
    19→          <GlobalStyle />
    20→          <AppRoutes />
    21→        </ThemeProvider>
    22→      </StoreProvider>
    23→    </Router>
    24→  )
    25→}
    26→

1→import { useLayoutEffect } from 'react'
     2→import { Routes, Route, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→
     4→import { CategoryListPage } from './pages/CategoryListPage'
     5→import { CategoryDetailPage } from './pages/CategoryDetailPage'
     6→import { RestaurantDetailPage } from './pages/RestaurantDetailPage'
     7→import { HomePage } from './pages/HomePage'
     8→import { CheckoutPage } from './pages/CheckoutPage'
     9→import { SuccessPage } from './pages/SuccessPage'
    10→
    11→export const AppRoutes: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<unknown>> = ({ children }) => {
    12→  const location = useLocation()
    13→  // Scroll to top when a path changes
    14→  useLayoutEffect(() => {
    15→    window.scrollTo(0, 0)
    16→  }, [location.pathname])
    17→
    18→  return (
    19→    <>
    20→      <div id="modal" />
    21→      <Routes>
    22→        <Route path="/categories" element={<CategoryListPage />} />
    23→        <Route path="/categories/:id" element={<CategoryDetailPage />} />
    24→        <Route path="/restaurants/:id" element={<RestaurantDetailPage />} />
    25→        <Route path="/checkout" element={<CheckoutPage />} />
    26→        <Route path="/success" element={<SuccessPage />} />
    27→        <Route path="/" element={<HomePage />} />
    28→        {children}
    29→      </Routes>
    30→    </>
    31→  )
    32→}
    33→

1→import { createSlice, CaseReducer, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { CartItem } from '../cart'
     4→
     5→export type OrderState = {
     6→  items: CartItem[]
     7→}
     8→
     9→const saveOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState, PayloadAction<CartItem[]>> = (state, action) => {
    10→  state.items = action.payload
    11→}
    12→
    13→const clearOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState> = (state) => {
    14→  state.items = []
    15→}
    16→
    17→const initialState: OrderState = {
    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→
    30→// Extract the action creators object and the reducer
    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice
    32→
    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→
    36→// Export the reducer, either as a default or named export
    37→export default reducer
    38→

1→import { createSelector } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { RootState } from '../store'
     4→
     5→const selectCart = (state: RootState) => state.cart
     6→
     7→export const selectCartVisibility = createSelector([selectCart], (cart) => cart.visible)
     8→
     9→export const selectCartItems = createSelector([selectCart], (cart) => cart.items)
    10→
    11→export const selectCartItemsCount = createSelector([selectCartItems], (items) =>
    12→  items.reduce((acc, item) => acc + item.quantity, 0)
    13→)
    14→
    15→export const selectCartTotal = createSelector([selectCartItems], (items) =>
    16→  items.reduce((acc, item) => acc + item.quantity * item.price, 0)

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export const useKey = (key: string, keyDownCb = () => {}, keyUpCb = () => {}): boolean => {
     4→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false)
     5→  const match = useCallback((event: any) => key.toLowerCase() === event.key.toLowerCase(), [key])
     6→
     7→  const onKeyDown = useCallback(
     8→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
     9→      if (match(event)) {
    10→        setPressed(true)
    11→        keyDownCb()
    12→      }
    13→    },
    14→    [keyDownCb, match]
    15→  )
    16→
    17→  const onKeyUp = useCallback(
    18→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
    19→      if (match(event)) {
    20→        setPressed(false)
    21→        keyUpCb()
    22→      }
    23→    },
    24→    [keyUpCb, match]
    25→  )
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    29→    globalThis.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    30→
    31→    return () => {
    32→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    33→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    34→    }
    35→  }, [key, onKeyDown, onKeyUp])
    36→
    37→  return pressed
    38→}
    39→

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the full report:

## Comprehensive Runtime Environment Analysis

### 1. ENTRY POINT (index.tsx)

**Location:** `/src/index.tsx`

Entry point creates a React root in the DOM element with id `#root` and renders the App component wrapped in React's StrictMode. Service workers are registered but kept unregistered by default.

### 2. APP.TSX - PROVIDER TREE & ROUTING

**Location:** `/src/App.tsx`

Provider stack from outermost to innermost:
1. **Router** (react-router-dom BrowserRouter)
2. **StoreProvider** (Redux store)
3. **ThemeProvider** (styled-components)
4. **GlobalStyle** (styled-components global styles)
5. **AppRoutes** (routing configuration)

Dark mode integration using `use-dark-mode` hook with `window` as global context.

### 3. ROUTES & PAGES

**Location:** `/src/Routes.tsx`

Routes defined:
- `/` - HomePage
- `/categories` - CategoryListPage  
- `/categories/:id` - CategoryDetailPage
- `/restaurants/:id` - RestaurantDetailPage
- `/checkout` - CheckoutPage
- `/success` - SuccessPage

Notable: Creates a `<div id="modal" />` portal root within Routes component.

**Pages:**
- **HomePage** - Displays banner, restaurant sections, award-winning section, categories
- **CategoryListPage** - Lists all categories with filtering
- **CategoryDetailPage** - Shows restaurants in a category with pagination
- **RestaurantDetailPage** - Restaurant menu with food sections (food, dessert, drinks)
- **CheckoutPage** - Multi-step form for order checkout
- **SuccessPage** - Order confirmation screen

### 4. PROVIDERS & CONTEXTS USED

**Redux Store** (`/src/app-state/`)
- **cart slice** - manages shopping cart state
  - `visible: boolean` - cart menu visibility
  - `items: CartItem[]` - items in cart with quantities
  - Actions: toggleVisibilityAction, saveItemAction, clearItemAction, removeItemAction, clearCartAction
  - Selectors: selectCartVisibility, selectCartItems, selectCartItemsCount, selectCartTotal

- **order slice** - manages order confirmation
  - `items: CartItem[]` - confirmed order items
  - Actions: saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction

**Typed Redux hooks:**
- `useAppDispatch()` - typed dispatch hook
- `useAppSelector()` - typed selector hook

**styled-components Theme** - lightTheme and darkTheme objects with:
- `color` - comprehensive color palette
- `spacing` - em-based spacing scale
- `typography` - font sizes and weights
- `borderRadius` - border radius scales
- `boxShadow` - shadow definitions
- `fonts` - font family definitions

### 5. CSS FILES & GLOBAL STYLES

**Location:** `/src/styles/GlobalStyle.tsx`

Imports:
- `react-multi-carousel/lib/styles.css`
- `react-loading-skeleton/dist/skeleton.css`

Global styles include:
- Montserrat and Hind Google Fonts (multiple weights)
- CSS reset (from CSSReset.ts)
- Smooth transitions (250ms on background, color, border)
- Container classes with responsive padding
- Footer and carousel overrides

### 6. DATA-FETCHING HOOKS & URLS

**Location:** `/src/api/hooks.ts`

**Hooks:**
1. **useFetchRestaurants()** - Fetches all restaurants
   - Returns: { status, restaurants, retryRequest }
   - Status codes: 'idle', 'loading', 'success', or HTTP error code string

2. **useFetchRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)** - Fetches restaurants by category
   - Conditional on categoryId being provided
   - Returns: { status, restaurants, retryRequest }

3. **useFetchRestaurant(id)** - Fetches single restaurant by ID
   - Returns: { status, restaurant, retryRequest }

**API Base URL:** `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`

**Query parameters:**
- `?id={id}` - Get single restaurant
- `?category={category}` - Get restaurants by category
- No params - Get all restaurants

**Implementation details:**
- Uses axios for HTTP requests
- Client-side caching (Map-based) disabled in test/Storybook environments
- Mocked environment detection via `import.meta.env.STORYBOOK || NODE_ENV === 'test'`
- Falls back to mock data (restaurantsCompleteData) in test environments

### 7. BROWSER STATE READS

**No localStorage/sessionStorage/cookies usage found.**

Custom browser API usage:
- **document.body.style.overflow** - Body scroll locking in `useLockBodyScroll` hook
- **document.body.style.height** - Mobile scroll prevention
- **globalThis.navigator.userAgent** - Mobile detection via `isMobile()` helper
- **window.scrollTo(0, 0)** - Scroll to top on route changes
- **document.querySelector()** - Portal element selection

### 8. PORTAL USAGE

**Two portal roots:**
1. **#root** - Main React application root (index.html)
2. **#modal** - Modal/overlay portal root (index.html + recreated in Routes.tsx)

**Portal implementations:**
- **Modal component** (`/src/components/Modal.tsx`) - Uses `<Portal selector="#modal">` 
  - Renders with CSSTransition animations
  - Locks body scroll when open
  - Escape key closes modal
  - Used in FoodItemModal for restaurant menu items

- **Custom Portal component** (`/src/components/Portal.tsx`)
  - Generic portal wrapper using React's createPortal
  - Queries DOM for selector and mounts children there

**Sidebar** (`/src/components/Sidebar/`) - NOT a portal but full-screen overlay
- Direct DOM rendering with CSSTransition
- Used for ShoppingCartMenu

### 9. COMPONENTS HIERARCHY

**Root layout components:**
- **PageTemplate** - Wraps pages with Header, Footer, content container
  - Props: type ('default' | 'sticky-header' | 'basic')
  - Default includes full Header + Footer
  - 'sticky-header' uses sticky positioning
  - 'basic' uses logo-only header

**Header Components:**
- **Header** - Connected Redux component managing cart state
- **HeaderComponent** - Presentational component
- **ShoppingCartMenu** - Cart sidebar (not a portal, overlays directly)
- **Logo, TopBanner** - Banner/logo components

**Shopping/Cart:**
- **ShoppingCart** - Main cart display component
- **ShoppingCartMenu** - Sidebar menu with items
- **ShoppingCartItem** - Individual item in cart
- **OrderSummary** - Order total and summary

**Forms:**
- **Input** - Text input component
- **Select** - Dropdown select component
- **MultiStepForm** - Multi-step checkout form (react-hooks-helper)
- **ContactDetails, DeliveryDetails, StepIndicator** - Form sections

**UI Components:**
- **Button** - Primary button with variants (clear, round, disabled, large, icon)
- **Badge** - Tag/badge component
- **Modal** - Full-screen modal with escape key support
- **Sidebar** - Side panel overlay
- **Spinner** - Loading spinner
- **ErrorBlock** - Error state with image and call-to-action
- **Breadcrumb** - Navigation breadcrumb
- **TopBanner** - Banner with photo and title
- **Icon, IconButton** - Icon rendering
- **Review** - Star rating display
- **AnimatedIllustration** - Lottie animation player
- **RestaurantCard, RestaurantCardSkeleton** - Restaurant card with skeleton loader
- **PageSection** - Content section wrapper
- **Footer, FooterCard** - Footer components

**Typography:**
- **Heading** - Heading component with levels 1-4
- **Body** - Body text component with variants
- **typography/index.tsx** - Typography exports

### 10. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
src/
├── index.tsx                 # Entry point
├── App.tsx                   # Root provider tree
├── Routes.tsx                # Routing configuration
├── api/
│   ├── index.ts              # Real API client
│   ├── conditional-logic.ts  # Mock API for tests
│   └── hooks.ts              # Data fetching hooks
├── app-state/
│   ├── store.ts              # Redux store config
│   ├── hooks.ts              # useAppDispatch, useAppSelector
│   ├── cart/
│   │   ├── cart.ts           # Cart slice
│   │   └── selectors.ts      # Cart selectors
│   └── order/
│       ├── order.ts          # Order slice
│       └── selectors.ts      # Order selectors
├── components/               # Reusable components
├── pages/                    # Page components
├── templates/
│   └── PageTemplate.tsx      # Layout template
├── styles/
│   ├── GlobalStyle.tsx       # Global styles
│   ├── theme.ts              # Theme definitions
│   ├── breakpoints.ts        # Media queries
│   └── CSSReset.ts           # CSS reset
├── hooks/
│   ├── useBodyScrollLock.ts  # Body scroll lock
│   └── useKeyboard.ts        # Keyboard event hook
├── helpers/
│   ├── getCurrency.ts        # Currency formatter
│   └── index.ts              # Utility functions
├── types/
│   └── index.ts              # TypeScript types
├── stub/
│   ├── restaurants.ts        # Mock restaurant data
│   ├── categories.ts         # Mock categories
│   └── cart-items.ts         # Mock cart items
├── assets/
├── docs/
└── stories/                  # Storybook stories
```

### 11. PACKAGE.JSON DEPENDENCIES

**Key dependencies:**
- **React 19.0.0** - UI library
- **react-dom 19.0.0** - DOM rendering
- **react-router-dom 7.2.0** - Routing
- **@reduxjs/toolkit 2.6.0** - State management
- **react-redux 9.2.0** - Redux bindings
- **styled-components 6.1.15** - CSS-in-JS
- **axios 1.8.1** - HTTP client
- **react-hook-form 7.54.2** - Form management
- **react-transition-group 4.4.5** - CSS transitions
- **react-loading-skeleton 3.5.0** - Skeleton loaders
- **react-multi-carousel 2.8.5** - Carousel component
- **react-lottie-player 2.1.0** - Lottie animations
- **use-dark-mode 2.3.1** - Dark mode hook
- **zod 3.24.2** - Schema validation
- **@hookform/resolvers 4.1.3** - Form validation
- **react-hooks-helper 1.6.0** - Multi-step form helper

**Dev dependencies:**
- **Storybook 0.0.0-pr-34421** - Component development environment
- **@storybook/react-vite** - Vite integration
- **vitest 4.0.15** - Test runner
- **@vitest/browser-playwright 4.0.15** - Browser testing
- **@testing-library/react 16.2.0** - React testing utilities
- **msw 2.7.3** - Mock Service Worker for API mocking
- **prettier 3.5.2** - Code formatting
- **eslint 9.21.0** - Linting
- **typescript 5.7.3** - TypeScript compiler
- **vite 6.2.0** - Build tool

### 12. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION

**Location:** `.storybook/`

**main.ts configuration:**
- Stories from: `../src/**/*.mdx`, `../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)`, `./eval-support/*.mdx`
- Framework: @storybook/react-vite
- Addons:
  - @chromatic-com/storybook (visual testing)
  - @storybook/addon-vitest (test integration)
  - @storybook/addon-a11y (accessibility)
  - @storybook/addon-docs (documentation)
  - @storybook/addon-onboarding (onboarding)

**preview.tsx configuration:**
- Control matchers for color and date properties
- a11y testing mode set to 'todo'

**Mock environment:** API uses MockedRestaurantsApi when `import.meta.env.STORYBOOK` is true

### 13. TYPESCRIPT & VITE CONFIGURATION

**tsconfig.json:**
- Target: ESNext
- Module: ESNext
- Strict mode enabled
- JSX: react-jsx (new JSX transform)
- Base URL: src (for absolute imports)
- Module resolution: bundler
- Types: vite/client, vitest/globals, @testing-library/jest-dom

**vite.config.ts:**
- React plugin enabled
- Build output: build/
- Dev server port: 3000
- No custom aliases or special config beyond React plugin

### 14. CUSTOM HOOKS

**useLockBodyScroll(shouldLock)** - Prevents body scrolling
- Sets `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'`
- Mobile-specific: sets `document.body.style.height = '100vh'`
- Cleanup restores scrolling on unmount

**useKey(key, keyDownCb, keyUpCb)** - Keyboard event listener
- Listens to globalThis for keydown/keyup events
- Case-insensitive key matching
- Used for Escape key in modals and sidebars
- Cleans up event listeners on unmount

### 15. DARK MODE

**Implementation:** `use-dark-mode` package
- Enabled in App.tsx with `useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })`
- Toggles between lightTheme and darkTheme from styled-components
- ThemeProvider switches theme throughout app
- UI toggle in Header via Button component

### 16. FORM HANDLING

**react-hook-form integration:**
- Used in CheckoutPage's MultiStepForm
- Form validation via zod schema
- resolver: @hookform/resolvers for zod validation
- Multi-step form using react-hooks-helper
- ContactDetails and DeliveryDetails form sections

### 17. ANIMATIONS & TRANSITIONS

**react-transition-group usage:**
- Modal entrance/exit animations (classNames: "modal", "backdrop")
- Sidebar entrance/exit animations (classNames: "sidebar", "backdrop")
- 300ms animation timeout
- CSSTransition with unmountOnExit

**Lottie animations:**
- AnimatedIllustration component uses react-lottie-player
- Used in error states for visual feedback

**Carousel:**
- react-multi-carousel for restaurant carousels
- Global CSS overrides in GlobalStyle

---

This is a fully-featured food delivery/restaurant browsing React application with comprehensive state management, routing, data fetching, and a complete UI component library. The architecture is well-structured with clear separation between presentational components, containers, pages, and state management layers.

- **prettier 3.5.2** - Code formatting
- **eslint 9.21.0** - Linting
- **typescript 5.7.3** - TypeScript compiler
- **vite 6.2.0** - Build tool

### 12. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION

**Location:** `.storybook/`

**main.ts configuration:**
- Stories from: `../src/**/*.mdx`, `../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)`, `./eval-support/*.mdx`
- Framework: @storybook/react-vite
- Addons:
  - @chromatic-com/storybook (visual testing)
  - @storybook/addon-vitest (test integration)
  - @storybook/addon-a11y (accessibility)
  - @storybook/addon-docs (documentation)
  - @storybook/addon-onboarding (onboarding)

**preview.tsx configuration:**
- Control matchers for color and date properties
- a11y testing mode set to 'todo'

**Mock environment:** API uses MockedRestaurantsApi when `import.meta.env.STORYBOOK` is true

### 13. TYPESCRIPT & VITE CONFIGURATION

**tsconfig.json:**
- Target: ESNext
- Module: ESNext
- Strict mode enabled
- JSX: react-jsx (new JSX transform)
- Base URL: src (for absolute imports)
- Module resolution: bundler
- Types: vite/client, vitest/globals, @testing-library/jest-dom

**vite.config.ts:**
- React plugin enabled
- Build output: build/
- Dev server port: 3000
- No custom aliases or special config beyond React plugin

### 14. CUSTOM HOOKS

**useLockBodyScroll(shouldLock)** - Prevents body scrolling
- Sets `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'`
- Mobile-specific: sets `document.body.style.height = '100vh'`
- Cleanup restores scrolling on unmount

**useKey(key, keyDownCb, keyUpCb)** - Keyboard event listener
- Listens to globalThis for keydown/keyup events
- Case-insensitive key matching
- Used for Escape key in modals and sidebars
- Cleans up event listeners on unmount

### 15. DARK MODE

**Implementation:** `use-dark-mode` package
- Enabled in App.tsx with `useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })`
- Toggles between lightTheme and darkTheme from styled-components
- ThemeProvider switches theme throughout app
- UI toggle in Header via Button component

### 16. FORM HANDLING

**react-hook-form integration:**
- Used in CheckoutPage's MultiStepForm
- Form validation via zod schema
- resolver: @hookform/resolvers for zod validation
- Multi-step form using react-hooks-helper
- ContactDetails and DeliveryDetails form sections

### 17. ANIMATIONS & TRANSITIONS

**react-transition-group usage:**
- Modal entrance/exit animations (classNames: "modal", "backdrop")
- Sidebar entrance/exit animations (classNames: "sidebar", "backdrop")
- 300ms animation timeout
- CSSTransition with unmountOnExit

**Lottie animations:**
- AnimatedIllustration component uses react-lottie-player
- Used in error states for visual feedback

**Carousel:**
- react-multi-carousel for restaurant carousels
- Global CSS overrides in GlobalStyle

---

This is a fully-featured food delivery/restaurant browsing React application with comprehensive state management, routing, data fetching, and a complete UI component library. The architecture is well-structured with clear separation between presentational components, containers, pages, and state management layers.

import useDarkMode from 'use-dark-mode'
import { ThemeProvider } from 'styled-components'
import { BrowserRouter as Router } from 'react-router-dom'
import { Provider as StoreProvider } from 'react-redux'

import { store } from './app-state'
import { AppRoutes } from './Routes'
import { lightTheme, darkTheme } from './styles/theme'
import { GlobalStyle } from './styles/GlobalStyle'

export default function App() {
  const { value } = useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })
  const theme = value ? darkTheme : lightTheme

  return (
    <Router>
      <StoreProvider store={store}>
        <ThemeProvider theme={theme}>
          <GlobalStyle />
          <AppRoutes />
        </ThemeProvider>
      </StoreProvider>
    </Router>
  )
}

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

import { DefaultTheme } from 'styled-components'

declare module 'styled-components' {
  export interface DefaultTheme {
    name: 'light' | 'dark'
    fonts: {
      family: string
    }
    spacing: typeof spacing
    color: typeof color
    borderRadius: typeof borderRadius
    boxShadow: typeof boxShadow
    typography: typeof typography
  }
}

const baseColors = {
  white: '#FFFFFF ',
  black: '#202020',
  red: {
    base: '#D70808',
    light1: '#E57373',
    light2: '#EF9A9A',
    dark1: '#C62828',
    dark2: '#B71C1C',
  },
  otherBlack: '#2C2C2C ',
  grey: {
    base: '#909090',
    light1: '#A6A6A6',
    light2: '#BCBCBC',
    light3: '#D2D2D2',
    light4: '#E9E9E9',
    light5: '#F5F6F7',
    light6: '#F9F9F9',
    dark1: '#797979',
    dark2: '#636363',
    dark3: '#4D4D4D',
    dark4: '#363636',
    dark5: '#2C2C2C',
    dark6: '#202020',
  },
  green: {
    base: '#E5F8BC',
    light1: '#E8F9C3',
    light2: '#EAF9C9',
    light3: '#EDFAD0',
    light4: '#EFFBD7',
    light5: '#F2FCDE',
    dark1: '#CEDFA9',
    dark2: '#B7C696',
    dark3: '#A0AE84',
    dark4: '#899571',
    dark5: '#737C5E',
  },
  blue: {
    base: '#B1DDE4',
    light1: '#B9E0E7',
    light2: '#C1E4E9',
    light3: '#C8E7EC',
    light4: '#D0EBEF',
    light5: '#D8EEF2',
    dark1: '#9FC7CD',
    dark2: '#8EB1B6',
    dark3: '#7C9BA0',
    dark4: '#6A8589',
    dark5: '#596f72',
  },
}

const spaceUnit = 1

const borderRadius = {
  xs: '4px',
  s: '8px',
  m: '16px',
  l: '24px',
  xl: '32px',
  xxl: '40px',
  round: '50%',
}

const spacing = {
  xxs: `${0.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xs: `${0.5 * spaceUnit}em`,
  s: `${spaceUnit}em`,
  m: `${1.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  l: `${2 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xl: `${3.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
  xxl: `${5.25 * spaceUnit}em`,
}

const boxShadow = {
  card: '0px 14px 26px 0px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.08)',
  inner: 'inset 0 3px 0 0 rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05)',
  outerBorder: `0 0 0 1px ${baseColors.blue.dark2}, 0 0 0 4px ${baseColors.blue.light5}`,
}

const typography = {
  fontSize: {
    body: '1.125rem',
    bodyS: '1rem',
    bodyXS: '0.9rem',
    bodyXXS: '0.72rem',
    heading1: '2.74rem',

}

const color = {

  cartButtonText: baseColors.grey.base,
  bannerBackground: baseColors.blue.base,
  buttonClear: 'transparent',
  buttonClearHover: baseColors.grey.light5,
  buttonPrimary: baseColors.grey.dark6,

  buttonSecondary: baseColors.green.base,
  buttonSecondaryHover: baseColors.green.dark1, // recheck
  buttonText: baseColors.white,
  cardBackground: baseColors.grey.light6,

  sidebarFooter: baseColors.white,

  white: baseColors.white,
  black: baseColors.black,
}

export const lightTheme: DefaultTheme = {
  borderRadius,
  boxShadow,
  color,
  fonts: {
    family: 'NunitoSans, sans-serif',
  },
  name: 'light',
  spacing,
  typography,
}

export const darkTheme: DefaultTheme = {
  ...lightTheme,
  boxShadow: {
    ...boxShadow,

    primaryText: baseColors.white,
    reviewText: baseColors.blue.base,
    screenBackground: baseColors.grey.dark6,
    sidebarHeader: baseColors.grey.dark4,
    sidebarFooter: baseColors.grey.dark5,
    skeletonBase: baseColors.grey.dark3,
    skeletonHighlight: baseColors.grey.dark2,
    topBannerBackground: baseColors.grey.dark5,
  },
  name: 'dark',
}

export type ShopItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  price: number
  imageUrl?: string
  description?: string
}

export type FoodMenuItem = {
  id: number
  name: string
  description?: string
  price: number
}

export type Restaurant = {
  name: string
  id?: string
  mapsUrl?: string
  rating?: number
  url?: string
  address?: string
  specialty: string
  photoUrl: string
  isClosed?: boolean
  categories?: string[]
  isLoading?: boolean
  isNew?: boolean
  menu: {
    food: FoodMenuItem[]
    dessert: FoodMenuItem[]
    drinks: FoodMenuItem[]
  }
}

import { Restaurant } from '../types'

const food = [
  {
    id: 1,
    name: 'Cheeseburger',
    description: 'Nice grilled burger with cheese',
    price: 8.5,
  },
  {
    id: 2,
    name: 'Fries',
    description: 'Fried french fries',
    price: 2.5,
  },
]

const dessert = [
  {
    id: 3,
    name: 'Vanilla ice cream',
    description: 'Ice cream',
    price: 2,
  },
]

    id: 4,

    price: 1.75,

    id: 5,
    name: 'Fanta',
    price: 1.5,

    id: 6,
    name: 'Sprite',

]

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import { CSSTransition } from 'react-transition-group'
     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'
     8→
     9→import { ModalContent, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Modal.styles'
    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}
    63→

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import styled, { css } from 'styled-components'
     3→
     4→type FontSize = 'body' | 'bodyS' | 'bodyXS' | 'bodyXXS'
     5→type FontWeight = 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
     6→
     7→type StyledBodyProps = {
     8→  $size: string
     9→  $fontWeight: string
    10→}
    11→
    12→const BodyBase = styled.p<StyledBodyProps>(
    13→  ({ $size, $fontWeight, color: textColor, theme: { typography, color } }) => css`
    14→    display: block;
    15→    font-family: 'Hind';
    16→    color: ${textColor || color.primaryText};
    17→    font-weight: ${typography.fontWeight[$fontWeight as FontWeight]};
    18→    font-size: ${typography.fontSize[`body${$size}` as FontSize]};
    19→  `
    20→)
    21→
    22→type AsElement = 'span' | 'p' | 'label' | 'figcaption'
    23→
    24→type ElementProps<T extends AsElement> = T extends 'label'
    25→  ? React.LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>
    26→  : T extends 'span'
    27→    ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>
    28→    : T extends 'p'
    29→      ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>
    30→      : React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>
    31→
    32→type DefaultProps = {
    33→  className?: string
    34→  size?: 'S' | 'XS' | 'XXS'
    35→  fontWeight?: 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
    36→  type?: AsElement
    37→  color?: string
    38→  children: React.ReactNode | string
    39→}
    40→
    41→type BodyProps = DefaultProps & ElementProps<AsElement>
    42→
    43→export const Body: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<BodyProps>> = ({
    44→  size = '',
    45→  fontWeight = 'regular',
    46→  type = 'p',
    47→  color,
    48→  children,
    49→  className,
    50→  ...props
    51→}) => (
    52→  <BodyBase
    53→    as={type}
    54→    $size={size}
    55→    $fontWeight={fontWeight}
    56→    color={color}
    57→    className={className}
    58→    {...props}
    59→  >
    60→    {children}
    61→  </BodyBase>
    62→)
    63→

import styled, { useTheme } from 'styled-components'

const StyledSVG = styled.svg`
  width: 100px;
  height: 100px;
  position: absolute;
  left: 50%;
  bottom: 50%;
  transform: translate(-50%, -50%);
`

export const Spinner = () => {
  const { color } = useTheme()
  return (
    <StyledSVG
      xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
      viewBox="0 0 100 100"
      preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid"
    >
      <text transform="translate(-50 0)" fill={color.primaryText}>
        Looking for some food...
      </text>
      <animate attributeName="opacity" dur="1s" keyTimes="0;0.4;1" values="0;0;1" />
      <g>
        <circle cx="60" cy="50" r="4" fill="#22aca7">
          <animate
            attributeName="cx"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="95;35"
            keyTimes="0;1"
            begin="-0.6566000000000001s"
          />
          <animate
            attributeName="fill-opacity"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="0;1;1"
            keyTimes="0;0.2;1"
            begin="-0.6566000000000001s"
          />
        </circle>
        <circle cx="60" cy="50" r="4" fill="#22aca7">
          <animate
            attributeName="cx"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="95;35"
            keyTimes="0;1"
            begin="-0.3234s"
          />
          <animate
            attributeName="fill-opacity"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="0;1;1"
            keyTimes="0;0.2;1"
            begin="-0.3234s"
          />
        </circle>
        <circle cx="60" cy="50" r="4" fill="#22aca7">
          <animate
            attributeName="cx"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="95;35"
            keyTimes="0;1"
            begin="0s"
          />
          <animate
            attributeName="fill-opacity"
            repeatCount="indefinite"
            dur="1.0204081632653061s"
            values="0;1;1"
            keyTimes="0;0.2;1"
            begin="0s"
          />
        </circle>
      </g>
      <g transform="translate(-15 0)">
        <path d="M50 50L20 50A30 30 0 0 0 80 50Z" fill="#61d8de" transform="rotate(90 50 50)" />
        <path d="M50 50L20 50A30 30 0 0 0 80 50Z" fill="#61d8de">
          <animateTransform

1→import styled from 'styled-components'
     2→import { useNavigate } from 'react-router-dom'
     3→import Carousel, { type ArrowProps, type CarouselProps } from 'react-multi-carousel'
     4→
     5→import { useFetchRestaurants } from '../../../../api/hooks'
     6→import { IconButton } from '../../../../components/IconButton'
     7→import { PageSection } from '../../../../components/PageSection'
     8→import { RestaurantCard, RestaurantCardSkeleton } from '../../../../components/RestaurantCard'
     9→import { Restaurant } from '../../../../types'
    10→import { isMobile } from '../../../../helpers'
    11→
    12→const PreviousButton = styled(IconButton)`
    13→  position: absolute;
    14→  left: 0;
    15→`
    16→
    17→const NextButton = styled(IconButton)`
    18→  position: absolute;
    19→  right: 0;
    20→`
    21→
    22→type RestaurantsSectionProps = {
    23→  title: string
    24→}
    25→
    26→const CustomArrow = (props: Partial<CarouselProps & ArrowProps> & { isNext?: boolean }) => {
    27→  // filter out unnecessary props coming from react-multi-carousel
    28→  // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-unused-vars
    29→  const { carouselState, rtl, isNext, ...rest } = props
    30→  return isNext ? (
    31→    <NextButton name="arrow-right" {...rest} />
    32→  ) : (
    33→    <PreviousButton name="arrow-left" {...rest} />
    34→  )
    35→}
    36→
    37→export const RestaurantsSection = ({ title }: RestaurantsSectionProps) => {
    38→  const navigate = useNavigate()
    39→
    40→  const { restaurants, status } = useFetchRestaurants()
    41→
    42→  const isOnMobile = isMobile()
    43→  return (
    44→    <PageSection title={title}>
    45→      <Carousel
    46→        draggable={isOnMobile}
    47→        partialVisible={isOnMobile}
    48→        customLeftArrow={<CustomArrow />}
    49→        customRightArrow={<CustomArrow isNext />}
    50→        responsive={{
    51→          desktop: {
    52→            breakpoint: { max: 5000, min: 1024 },
    53→            items: 3,
    54→            slidesToSlide: 3,
    55→          },
    56→          tablet: {
    57→            breakpoint: { max: 1024, min: 464 },
    58→            items: 2,
    59→            paritialVisibilityGutter: 50,
    60→          },
    61→          mobile: {
    62→            breakpoint: { max: 464, min: 0 },
    63→            items: 1,
    64→            paritialVisibilityGutter: 30,
    65→          },
    66→        }}
    67→        containerClass="carousel-container"
    68→        removeArrowOnDeviceType={['tablet', 'mobile']}
    69→        itemClass="carousel-item"
    70→      >
    71→        {status === 'loading'
    72→          ? Array.from({ length: 3 }).map((_, index) => <RestaurantCardSkeleton key={index} />)
    73→          : restaurants.map((restaurant: Restaurant, index: number) => (
    74→              <RestaurantCard
    75→                key={restaurant.name + index}
    76→                {...restaurant}
    77→                onClick={() => navigate(`/restaurants/${restaurant.id}`)}
    78→              />
    79→            ))}
    80→      </Carousel>
    81→    </PageSection>
    82→  )
    83→}

   - `<StoreProvider store={store}>` (Redux)
   - `<ThemeProvider theme={theme}>` (styled-components)
   - `<GlobalStyle />` (global CSS)
   - `<AppRoutes />` (routes)

3. **API**: Uses axios to fetch from `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`. 

I'm noticing there's a mismatch between the conditional logic in `src/api/conditional-logic.ts` that switches to `MockedRestaurantsApi` in Storybook mode, but the actual `src/api/index.ts` always uses the real `RestaurantsApi` class instead. content.thinking:
I notice a potential issue with the Portal component - it uses `useRef` and `useEffect` to find the element on mount. The first render will always return `null` because `ref.current` is null initially and `useEffect` runs after render. This means the portal will never actually render content because `ref.current` is set in useEffect but no re-render is triggered.

Wait, actually this is a bug in the Portal component - it sets `ref.current` in useEffect but doesn't trigger a re-render. So the modal would never show up. But the app presumably works, so maybe there's a workaround or I'm missing something.


Actually, I see how this could work - the Portal returns null on first render, then useEffect sets the ref, and when the Modal's `isOpen` state changes later, that triggers a re-render where `ref.current` is now available. So the key requirement is that the `#modal` div needs to already exist in the DOM before the Portal component mounts.

Now I'm going to set up all the necessary files: the Storybook configuration with static directories, MSW handlers for mocking API calls, the preview file with the full provider tree and MSW setup, the portal root in the preview body HTML, and then write out all the story files.

Setting up configuration files first...

content.thinking:
The stories with required props need `args` provided even when using `render`. The Storybook typing requires `args` when the component has required props. I need to add `args` to these stories or change the approach.

Looking at the error, when a component has required props (like `text` for Badge, `title` and `photoUrl` for Category, `name`, `specialty`, `photoUrl` for RestaurantCard), Storybook requires `args` to be provided.

I have two options:
1. Add `args` to each story alongside `render`
2. Use `args` instead of `render` where possible


3. Remove the `component` from the meta to avoid the type inference

The cleanest solution is to provide `args` with the required props alongside the `render` functions, which satisfies Storybook's type requirements while keeping the render logic intact.

1→import * as React from 'react'
     2→import styled, { css } from 'styled-components'
     3→
     4→type FontSize = 'body' | 'bodyS' | 'bodyXS' | 'bodyXXS'
     5→type FontWeight = 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
     6→
     7→type StyledBodyProps = {
     8→  $size: string
     9→  $fontWeight: string
    10→}
    11→
    12→const BodyBase = styled.p<StyledBodyProps>(
    13→  ({ $size, $fontWeight, color: textColor, theme: { typography, color } }) => css`
    14→    display: block;
    15→    font-family: 'Hind';
    16→    color: ${textColor || color.primaryText};
    17→    font-weight: ${typography.fontWeight[$fontWeight as FontWeight]};
    18→    font-size: ${typography.fontSize[`body${$size}` as FontSize]};
    19→  `
    20→)
    21→
    22→type AsElement = 'span' | 'p' | 'label' | 'figcaption'
    23→
    24→type ElementProps<T extends AsElement> = T extends 'label'
    25→  ? React.LabelHTMLAttributes<HTMLLabelElement>
    26→  : T extends 'span'
    27→    ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLSpanElement>
    28→    : T extends 'p'
    29→      ? React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLParagraphElement>
    30→      : React.HTMLAttributes<HTMLElement>
    31→
    32→type DefaultProps = {
    33→  className?: string
    34→  size?: 'S' | 'XS' | 'XXS'
    35→  fontWeight?: 'regular' | 'medium' | 'bold' | 'black'
    36→  type?: AsElement
    37→  color?: string
    38→  children: React.ReactNode | string
    39→}
    40→
    41→type BodyProps = DefaultProps & ElementProps<AsElement>
    42→
    43→export const Body: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<BodyProps>> = ({
    44→  size = '',
    45→  fontWeight = 'regular',
    46→  type = 'p',
    47→  color,
    48→  children,
    49→  className,
    50→  ...props
    51→}) => (
    52→  <BodyBase
    53→    as={type}
    54→    $size={size}
    55→    $fontWeight={fontWeight}
    56→    color={color}
    57→    className={className}
    58→    {...props}
    59→  >
    60→    {children}
    61→  </BodyBase>
    62→)
    63→

content.thinking:
No console errors beyond the empty render check. Let me check if the GlobalStyle's transition is the culprit. The transition CSS `* { transition: all 250ms ease-in; transition-property: background, color, border; }` affects ALL elements.

When the styled-components inject CSS, the initial style injection might cause a transition from the default styles to the styled styles. During this transition period (250ms), the colors might be in an intermediate state that the empty render check considers invisible.

Let me try removing GlobalStyle from the preview and see if that fixes it.

Actually wait, let me think about what happens:
1. The browser renders the DOM (text is there, with browser default styles - black text)


2. styled-components injects the CSS styles
3. The `*` selector's transition triggers, transitioning `color` from #000000 to #2c2c2c over 250ms
4. But #000000 (black) is visible, and #2c2c2c is also visible, so the text should be visible throughout

That doesn't explain it. The real issue might be that the transition applies to all elements including the body background—when styled-components injects the white background, the transition from transparent to white could be creating a visual state where the text contrast is momentarily compromised during the 250ms animation.

Let me test this by removing the GlobalStyle entirely to see if the empty render issue goes away, or better yet, create a minimal test case without styled-components to isolate whether the transition itself is the culprit.

Actually, I'm wondering if the MemoryRouter in the preview decorator is the real problem here—maybe it's conflicting with how the Badge component renders in the story. Let me try stripping preview.tsx down to its bare essentials and see if that clears things up.

content.text: Let me test with a minimal preview to isolate the issue.

content.thinking:
The nested MemoryRouter issue - the preview has a MemoryRouter and the page story decorators also have a MemoryRouter. I need to override the preview's MemoryRouter for these page stories. In their decorators, I should replace the MemoryRouter instead of nesting it.

The solution: In the page story decorators, I should not add a MemoryRouter. Instead, I should use the preview's MemoryRouter but configure the initial entries differently. 

Actually, the simplest approach is to not nest routers. For page stories that need route params, I need to replace the preview's MemoryRouter. In Storybook, I can do this by:


- Providing a new wrapper that replaces the preview decorator, or
- Using `parameters` to pass route info and have the preview decorator use it

I'll go with the second approach: modify the preview decorator to accept route configuration via parameters, and have page stories specify their routes. But wait, the preview decorator creates MemoryRouter with no special route setup, and the pages use `useParams` which requires a `<Route path="...">` wrapper that the preview's MemoryRouter doesn't have. Let me rethink this - I should modify the preview decorator to check for route parameters and set up the necessary Route wrappers accordingly.

I'm creating a preview decorator that reads `routePath` and `routeEntry` from the story parameters, then wraps the story in a MemoryRouter with Routes configured based on those parameters. The page stories would then just specify their route path and initial entry point in the parameters object. This keeps the routing setup centralized in the preview and avoids nested routers.

content.text: Let me spot-check a few key screenshots.

All screenshots look great - the RestaurantCard shows the full card with image, rating, categories and badges. The HomePage shows the banner, header, and restaurant carousel. The RestaurantDetailPage shows the full page with header, restaurant photo, details, and menu items with prices.

Everything is working. Let me do a final summary of what was created:

**Storybook Configuration:**
- `.storybook/main.ts` - Added `staticDirs: ['../public']` for MSW service worker
- `.storybook/preview.tsx` - Full provider tree (MemoryRouter, Redux StoreProvider, ThemeProvider, GlobalStyle) + MSW setup with route parameter support
- `.storybook/preview-body.html` - Portal root `<div id="modal">` for modal components
- `.storybook/msw-handlers.ts` - MSW handlers intercepting all 3 API endpoints (all restaurants, by ID, by category)

**10 Story Files (29 story exports):**
1. `src/components/Button/Button.stories.tsx` - 7 stories (Default, Clear, Large, WithIcon, Disabled, Round, IconOnly)
2. `src/components/Badge/Badge.stories.tsx` - 3 stories (Default, ComfortFood, Pizza)
3. `src/components/Review/Review.stories.tsx` - 3 stories (VeryGood, Excellent, NoReviews)
4. `src/components/RestaurantCard/RestaurantCard.stories.tsx` - 4 stories (Default, New, Closed, Loading)
5. `src/components/Category/Category.stories.tsx` - 3 stories (Squared, Rounded, Sushi)
6. `src/components/TopBanner/TopBanner.stories.tsx` - 3 stories (WithTitle, WithPhoto, TitleOnly)
7. `src/components/Header/Header.stories.tsx` - 3 stories (Default, LogoOnly, WithCartItems)
8. `src/pages/HomePage/HomePage.stories.tsx` - 1 story (full page with data fetching via MSW)
9. `src/pages/RestaurantDetailPage/RestaurantDetailPage.stories.tsx` - 1 story (routed page with params)
10. `src/pages/CategoryDetailPage/CategoryDetailPage.stories.tsx` - 1 story (routed page with params)

All 37 tests pass, types are clean, and screenshots render correctly.

    23→    </Router>
    24→  )
    25→}

1→import { createSlice, CaseReducer, PayloadAction } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { CartItem } from '../cart'
     4→
     5→export type OrderState = {
     6→  items: CartItem[]
     7→}
     8→
     9→const saveOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState, PayloadAction<CartItem[]>> = (state, action) => {
    10→  state.items = action.payload
    11→}
    12→
    13→const clearOrder: CaseReducer<OrderState> = (state) => {
    14→  state.items = []
    15→}
    16→
    17→const initialState: OrderState = {
    18→  items: [],
    19→}
    20→
    21→const orderSlice = createSlice({
    22→  name: 'order',
    23→  initialState,
    24→  reducers: {
    25→    saveOrderAction: saveOrder,
    26→    clearOrderAction: clearOrder,
    27→  },
    28→})
    29→
    30→// Extract the action creators object and the reducer
    31→export const { actions, reducer } = orderSlice
    32→
    33→// Extract and export each action creator by name
    34→export const { saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction } = actions
    35→
    36→// Export the reducer, either as a default or named export
    37→export default reducer

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }

    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}

    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}

    17→export const Sidebar: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<SidebarProps>> = ({

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→export const useKey = (key: string, keyDownCb = () => {}, keyUpCb = () => {}): boolean => {
     4→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false)
     5→  const match = useCallback((event: any) => key.toLowerCase() === event.key.toLowerCase(), [key])
     6→
     7→  const onKeyDown = useCallback(
     8→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
     9→      if (match(event)) {
    10→        setPressed(true)
    11→        keyDownCb()
    12→      }
    13→    },
    14→    [keyDownCb, match]
    15→  )
    16→
    17→  const onKeyUp = useCallback(
    18→    (event: KeyboardEvent) => {
    19→      if (match(event)) {
    20→        setPressed(false)
    21→        keyUpCb()
    22→      }
    23→    },
    24→    [keyUpCb, match]
    25→  )
    26→
    27→  useEffect(() => {
    28→    globalThis.addEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    29→    globalThis.addEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    30→
    31→    return () => {
    32→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keydown', onKeyDown)
    33→      globalThis.removeEventListener('keyup', onKeyUp)
    34→    }
    35→  }, [key, onKeyDown, onKeyUp])
    36→
    37→  return pressed
    38→}

content.text:
Excellent! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me compile the full report:

## Comprehensive Runtime Environment Analysis

### 1. ENTRY POINT (index.tsx)

**Location:** `/src/index.tsx`

Entry point creates a React root in the DOM element with id `#root` and renders the App component wrapped in React's StrictMode. Service workers are registered but kept unregistered by default.

### 2. APP.TSX - PROVIDER TREE & ROUTING

**Location:** `/src/App.tsx`

Provider stack from outermost to innermost:
1. **Router** (react-router-dom BrowserRouter)
2. **StoreProvider** (Redux store)
3. **ThemeProvider** (styled-components)
4. **GlobalStyle** (styled-components global styles)
5. **AppRoutes** (routing configuration)

Dark mode integration using `use-dark-mode` hook with `window` as global context.

### 3. ROUTES & PAGES

**Location:** `/src/Routes.tsx`

Routes defined:
- `/` - HomePage
- `/categories` - CategoryListPage  
- `/categories/:id` - CategoryDetailPage
- `/restaurants/:id` - RestaurantDetailPage
- `/checkout` - CheckoutPage
- `/success` - SuccessPage

Notable: Creates a `<div id="modal" />` portal root within Routes component.

**Pages:**
- **HomePage** - Displays banner, restaurant sections, award-winning section, categories
- **CategoryListPage** - Lists all categories with filtering
- **CategoryDetailPage** - Shows restaurants in a category with pagination
- **RestaurantDetailPage** - Restaurant menu with food sections (food, dessert, drinks)
- **CheckoutPage** - Multi-step form for order checkout
- **SuccessPage** - Order confirmation screen

### 4. PROVIDERS & CONTEXTS USED

**Redux Store** (`/src/app-state/`)
- **cart slice** - manages shopping cart state
  - `visible: boolean` - cart menu visibility
  - `items: CartItem[]` - items in cart with quantities
  - Actions: toggleVisibilityAction, saveItemAction, clearItemAction, removeItemAction, clearCartAction
  - Selectors: selectCartVisibility, selectCartItems, selectCartItemsCount, selectCartTotal

- **order slice** - manages order confirmation
  - `items: CartItem[]` - confirmed order items
  - Actions: saveOrderAction, clearOrderAction

**Typed Redux hooks:**
- `useAppDispatch()` - typed dispatch hook
- `useAppSelector()` - typed selector hook

**styled-components Theme** - lightTheme and darkTheme objects with:
- `color` - comprehensive color palette
- `spacing` - em-based spacing scale
- `typography` - font sizes and weights
- `borderRadius` - border radius scales
- `boxShadow` - shadow definitions
- `fonts` - font family definitions

### 5. CSS FILES & GLOBAL STYLES

**Location:** `/src/styles/GlobalStyle.tsx`

Imports:
- `react-multi-carousel/lib/styles.css`
- `react-loading-skeleton/dist/skeleton.css`

Global styles include:
- Montserrat and Hind Google Fonts (multiple weights)
- CSS reset (from CSSReset.ts)
- Smooth transitions (250ms on background, color, border)
- Container classes with responsive padding
- Footer and carousel overrides

### 6. DATA-FETCHING HOOKS & URLS

**Location:** `/src/api/hooks.ts`

**Hooks:**
1. **useFetchRestaurants()** - Fetches all restaurants
   - Returns: { status, restaurants, retryRequest }
   - Status codes: 'idle', 'loading', 'success', or HTTP error code string

2. **useFetchRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)** - Fetches restaurants by category
   - Conditional on categoryId being provided
   - Returns: { status, restaurants, retryRequest }

3. **useFetchRestaurant(id)** - Fetches single restaurant by ID
   - Returns: { status, restaurant, retryRequest }

**API Base URL:** `https://mealdrop.netlify.app/.netlify/functions/restaurants`

**Query parameters:**
- `?id={id}` - Get single restaurant
- `?category={category}` - Get restaurants by category
- No params - Get all restaurants

**Implementation details:**
- Uses axios for HTTP requests
- Client-side caching (Map-based) disabled in test/Storybook environments
- Mocked environment detection via `import.meta.env.STORYBOOK || NODE_ENV === 'test'`
- Falls back to mock data (restaurantsCompleteData) in test environments

### 7. BROWSER STATE READS

**No localStorage/sessionStorage/cookies usage found.**

Custom browser API usage:
- **document.body.style.overflow** - Body scroll locking in `useLockBodyScroll` hook
- **document.body.style.height** - Mobile scroll prevention
- **globalThis.navigator.userAgent** - Mobile detection via `isMobile()` helper
- **window.scrollTo(0, 0)** - Scroll to top on route changes
- **document.querySelector()** - Portal element selection

### 8. PORTAL USAGE

**Two portal roots:**
1. **#root** - Main React application root (index.html)
2. **#modal** - Modal/overlay portal root (index.html + recreated in Routes.tsx)

**Portal implementations:**
- **Modal component** (`/src/components/Modal.tsx`) - Uses `<Portal selector="#modal">` 
  - Renders with CSSTransition animations
  - Locks body scroll when open
  - Escape key closes modal
  - Used in FoodItemModal for restaurant menu items

- **Custom Portal component** (`/src/components/Portal.tsx`)
  - Generic portal wrapper using React's createPortal
  - Queries DOM for selector and mounts children there

**Sidebar** (`/src/components/Sidebar/`) - NOT a portal but full-screen overlay
- Direct DOM rendering with CSSTransition
- Used for ShoppingCartMenu

### 9. COMPONENTS HIERARCHY

**Root layout components:**
- **PageTemplate** - Wraps pages with Header, Footer, content container
  - Props: type ('default' | 'sticky-header' | 'basic')
  - Default includes full Header + Footer
  - 'sticky-header' uses sticky positioning
  - 'basic' uses logo-only header

**Header Components:**
- **Header** - Connected Redux component managing cart state
- **HeaderComponent** - Presentational component
- **ShoppingCartMenu** - Cart sidebar (not a portal, overlays directly)
- **Logo, TopBanner** - Banner/logo components

**Shopping/Cart:**
- **ShoppingCart** - Main cart display component
- **ShoppingCartMenu** - Sidebar menu with items
- **ShoppingCartItem** - Individual item in cart
- **OrderSummary** - Order total and summary

**Forms:**
- **Input** - Text input component
- **Select** - Dropdown select component
- **MultiStepForm** - Multi-step checkout form (react-hooks-helper)
- **ContactDetails, DeliveryDetails, StepIndicator** - Form sections

**UI Components:**
- **Button** - Primary button with variants (clear, round, disabled, large, icon)
- **Badge** - Tag/badge component
- **Modal** - Full-screen modal with escape key support
- **Sidebar** - Side panel overlay
- **Spinner** - Loading spinner
- **ErrorBlock** - Error state with image and call-to-action
- **Breadcrumb** - Navigation breadcrumb
- **TopBanner** - Banner with photo and title
- **Icon, IconButton** - Icon rendering
- **Review** - Star rating display
- **AnimatedIllustration** - Lottie animation player
- **RestaurantCard, RestaurantCardSkeleton** - Restaurant card with skeleton loader
- **PageSection** - Content section wrapper
- **Footer, FooterCard** - Footer components

**Typography:**
- **Heading** - Heading component with levels 1-4
- **Body** - Body text component with variants
- **typography/index.tsx** - Typography exports

### 10. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
src/
├── index.tsx                 # Entry point
├── App.tsx                   # Root provider tree
├── Routes.tsx                # Routing configuration
├── api/
│   ├── index.ts              # Real API client
│   ├── conditional-logic.ts  # Mock API for tests
│   └── hooks.ts              # Data fetching hooks
├── app-state/
│   ├── store.ts              # Redux store config
│   ├── hooks.ts              # useAppDispatch, useAppSelector
│   ├── cart/
│   │   ├── cart.ts           # Cart slice
│   │   └── selectors.ts      # Cart selectors
│   └── order/
│       ├── order.ts          # Order slice
│       └── selectors.ts      # Order selectors
├── components/               # Reusable components
├── pages/                    # Page components
├── templates/
│   └── PageTemplate.tsx      # Layout template
├── styles/
│   ├── GlobalStyle.tsx       # Global styles
│   ├── theme.ts              # Theme definitions
│   ├── breakpoints.ts        # Media queries
│   └── CSSReset.ts           # CSS reset
├── hooks/
│   ├── useBodyScrollLock.ts  # Body scroll lock
│   └── useKeyboard.ts        # Keyboard event hook
├── helpers/
│   ├── getCurrency.ts        # Currency formatter
│   └── index.ts              # Utility functions
├── types/
│   └── index.ts              # TypeScript types
├── stub/
│   ├── restaurants.ts        # Mock restaurant data
│   ├── categories.ts         # Mock categories
│   └── cart-items.ts         # Mock cart items
├── assets/
├── docs/
└── stories/                  # Storybook stories
```

### 11. PACKAGE.JSON DEPENDENCIES

**Key dependencies:**
- **React 19.0.0** - UI library
- **react-dom 19.0.0** - DOM rendering
- **react-router-dom 7.2.0** - Routing
- **@reduxjs/toolkit 2.6.0** - State management
- **react-redux 9.2.0** - Redux bindings
- **styled-components 6.1.15** - CSS-in-JS
- **axios 1.8.1** - HTTP client
- **react-hook-form 7.54.2** - Form management
- **react-transition-group 4.4.5** - CSS transitions
- **react-loading-skeleton 3.5.0** - Skeleton loaders
- **react-multi-carousel 2.8.5** - Carousel component
- **react-lottie-player 2.1.0** - Lottie animations
- **use-dark-mode 2.3.1** - Dark mode hook
- **zod 3.24.2** - Schema validation
- **@hookform/resolvers 4.1.3** - Form validation
- **react-hooks-helper 1.6.0** - Multi-step form helper

**Dev dependencies:**
- **Storybook 0.0.0-pr-34421** - Component development environment
- **@storybook/react-vite** - Vite integration
- **vitest 4.0.15** - Test runner
- **@vitest/browser-playwright 4.0.15** - Browser testing
- **@testing-library/react 16.2.0** - React testing utilities
- **msw 2.7.3** - Mock Service Worker for API mocking
- **prettier 3.5.2** - Code formatting
- **eslint 9.21.0** - Linting
- **typescript 5.7.3** - TypeScript compiler
- **vite 6.2.0** - Build tool

### 12. STORYBOOK CONFIGURATION

**Location:** `.storybook/`

**main.ts configuration:**
- Stories from: `../src/**/*.mdx`, `../src/**/*.stories.@(js|jsx|mjs|ts|tsx)`, `./eval-support/*.mdx`
- Framework: @storybook/react-vite
- Addons:
  - @chromatic-com/storybook (visual testing)
  - @storybook/addon-vitest (test integration)
  - @storybook/addon-a11y (accessibility)
  - @storybook/addon-docs (documentation)
  - @storybook/addon-onboarding (onboarding)

**preview.tsx configuration:**
- Control matchers for color and date properties
- a11y testing mode set to 'todo'

**Mock environment:** API uses MockedRestaurantsApi when `import.meta.env.STORYBOOK` is true

### 13. TYPESCRIPT & VITE CONFIGURATION

**tsconfig.json:**
- Target: ESNext
- Module: ESNext
- Strict mode enabled
- JSX: react-jsx (new JSX transform)
- Base URL: src (for absolute imports)
- Module resolution: bundler
- Types: vite/client, vitest/globals, @testing-library/jest-dom

**vite.config.ts:**
- React plugin enabled
- Build output: build/
- Dev server port: 3000
- No custom aliases or special config beyond React plugin

### 14. CUSTOM HOOKS

**useLockBodyScroll(shouldLock)** - Prevents body scrolling
- Sets `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'`
- Mobile-specific: sets `document.body.style.height = '100vh'`
- Cleanup restores scrolling on unmount

**useKey(key, keyDownCb, keyUpCb)** - Keyboard event listener
- Listens to globalThis for keydown/keyup events
- Case-insensitive key matching
- Used for Escape key in modals and sidebars
- Cleans up event listeners on unmount

### 15. DARK MODE

**Implementation:** `use-dark-mode` package
- Enabled in App.tsx with `useDarkMode(false, { global: globalThis.window })`
- Toggles between lightTheme and darkTheme from styled-components
- ThemeProvider switches theme throughout app
- UI toggle in Header via Button component

### 16. FORM HANDLING

**react-hook-form integration:**
- Used in CheckoutPage's MultiStepForm
- Form validation via zod schema
- resolver: @hookform/resolvers for zod validation
- Multi-step form using react-hooks-helper
- ContactDetails and DeliveryDetails form sections

### 17. ANIMATIONS & TRANSITIONS

**react-transition-group usage:**
- Modal entrance/exit animations (classNames: "modal", "backdrop")
- Sidebar entrance/exit animations (classNames: "sidebar", "backdrop")
- 300ms animation timeout
- CSSTransition with unmountOnExit

**Lottie animations:**
- AnimatedIllustration component uses react-lottie-player
- Used in error states for visual feedback

**Carousel:**
- react-multi-carousel for restaurant carousels
- Global CSS overrides in GlobalStyle

---

This is a fully-featured food delivery/restaurant browsing React application with comprehensive state management, routing, data fetching, and a complete UI component library. The architecture is well-structured with clear separation between presentational components, containers, pages, and state management layers.

1→import { useCallback, useEffect, useState } from 'react'
     2→
     3→import type { Restaurant } from '../types'
     4→
     5→import { api } from './index'
     6→
     7→export const useFetchRestaurants = () => {
     8→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
     9→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    10→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    11→
    12→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    13→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    14→  }, [setRetry])
    15→
    16→  useEffect(() => {
    17→    setStatus('loading')
    18→
    19→    api
    20→      .getRestaurants()
    21→      .then((res) => {
    22→        setStatus('success')
    23→        setRestaurants(res)
    24→      })
    25→      .catch((error) => {
    26→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    27→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    28→      })
    29→  }, [retry])
    30→
    31→  return {
    32→    status,
    33→    restaurants,
    34→    retryRequest,
    35→  }
    36→}
    37→
    38→export const useFetchRestaurantsByCategory = (categoryId?: string) => {
    39→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    40→  const [restaurants, setRestaurants] = useState<Restaurant[]>([])
    41→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    42→
    43→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    44→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    45→  }, [setRetry])
    46→
    47→  useEffect(() => {
    48→    if (categoryId) {
    49→      setStatus('loading')
    50→
    51→      api
    52→        .getRestaurantsByCategory(categoryId)
    53→        .then((res) => {
    54→          setStatus('success')
    55→          setRestaurants(res)
    56→        })
    57→        .catch((error) => {
    58→          const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    59→          setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    60→        })
    61→    }
    62→  }, [categoryId, retry])
    63→
    64→  return {
    65→    status,
    66→    restaurants,
    67→    retryRequest,
    68→  }
    69→}
    70→
    71→export const useFetchRestaurant = (id: string) => {
    72→  const [status, setStatus] = useState('idle')
    73→  const [restaurant, setRestaurant] = useState<Restaurant>()
    74→  const [retry, setRetry] = useState(false)
    75→
    76→  const retryRequest = useCallback(() => {
    77→    setRetry((prev) => !prev)
    78→  }, [setRetry])
    79→
    80→  useEffect(() => {
    81→    setStatus('loading')
    82→
    83→    api
    84→      .getRestaurantById(id)
    85→      .then((res) => {
    86→        setStatus('success')
    87→        setRestaurant(res)
    88→      })
    89→      .catch((error) => {
    90→        const statusCode = error?.response?.status || 500
    91→        setStatus(statusCode.toString())
    92→      })
    93→  }, [id, retry])
    94→
    95→  return {
    96→    status,
    97→    restaurant,
    98→    retryRequest,
    99→  }
   100→}
   101→

1→import { combineReducers, configureStore } from '@reduxjs/toolkit'
     2→
     3→import { reducer as cart } from './cart'
     4→import { reducer as order } from './order'
     5→
     6→export const rootReducer = combineReducers({
     7→  cart,
     8→  order,
     9→})
    10→
    11→export const store = configureStore({
    12→  reducer: rootReducer,
    13→})
    14→
    15→export type AppDispatch = typeof store.dispatch
    16→export type RootState = ReturnType<typeof store.getState>
    17→

    12→    "prettier": "prettier --write .",
    13→    "serve": "vite preview",

1→import * as React from 'react'

     3→
     4→import { Portal } from '../Portal'
     5→import { useKey } from '../../hooks/useKeyboard'
     6→import { Button } from '../Button'
     7→import { useLockBodyScroll } from '../../hooks/useBodyScrollLock'

     9→import { ModalContent, TopBar, Backdrop } from './Modal.styles'
    10→
    11→type ModalProps = {
    12→  isOpen: boolean
    13→  onClose: () => void
    14→}
    15→
    16→export const Modal: React.FC<React.PropsWithChildren<ModalProps>> = ({
    17→  children,
    18→  isOpen,
    19→  onClose,
    20→}) => {
    21→  useKey('escape', onClose)
    22→  useLockBodyScroll(isOpen)
    23→  const contentRef = React.useRef(null)
    24→  const backdropRef = React.useRef(null)
    25→
    26→  return (
    27→    <Portal selector="#modal">
    28→      <CSSTransition
    29→        in={isOpen}
    30→        timeout={300}
    31→        classNames="modal"
    32→        unmountOnExit
    33→        nodeRef={contentRef}
    34→      >
    35→        <ModalContent data-testid="modal" ref={contentRef}>
    36→          <TopBar>
    37→            <Button
    38→              data-testid="modal-close-btn"
    39→              onClick={onClose}
    40→              clear
    41→              round
    42→              icon="cross"
    43→              aria-label="close modal"
    44→              iconSize={16}
    45→            />
    46→          </TopBar>
    47→          {children}
    48→        </ModalContent>
    49→      </CSSTransition>
    50→
    51→      <CSSTransition
    52→        in={isOpen}
    53→        timeout={300}
    54→        classNames="backdrop"
    55→        unmountOnExit
    56→        nodeRef={backdropRef}
    57→      >
    58→        <Backdrop data-testid="modal-backdrop" ref={backdropRef} onClick={onClose} />
    59→      </CSSTransition>
    60→    </Portal>
    61→  )
    62→}
